hero.label: Software Engineer · Zurich
hero.sub: I build systems — from ESP32 firmware to Kubernetes clusters. Precise, pragmatic, with an eye for the bigger picture.

it.text: UZH, UBS, KPMG, AMZ Racing — from mainframe to Kubernetes.

rowing.text: President RCRJ. Safety Chief, National Rowing Regatta Schmerikon.

projects.title: Projects
projects.text: IoT platform, homelab k3s cluster, AI-powered club assistant.

automation.title: AI Automation
automation.text: I automate manual processes in manufacturing SMEs — in weeks.

about.label: About me
about.p1: I combine technical depth with organisational leadership — equally at home in a Kubernetes cluster and a board meeting.
about.p2: Currently: BSc Software Systems at UZH, SoPra FS26 (Karaokee), homelab infrastructure with Ansible + k3s, club management tool for RCRJ.

cta.togsub: I welcome interesting projects — from infrastructure to fullstack.

about.headerSub: Three roles, one common thread: building systems that work in real life.

it.headerSub: Professional experience in major banks, consulting, Formula Student and my own studies.

rowing.headerTitle: 200 members. A national regatta. A CHF 5M project.
rowing.headerSub: Voluntary — and at least as instructive as any computer-science module.

projects.headerTitle: What I build — from sensors to servers.
projects.headerSub: Open-source where possible. Infrastructure as Code. End-to-end.

automation.heroSub: I automate them — in weeks, not months. No big project, no system swap, measurable ROI from month one.
automation.problem1Text: Delivery notes, orders and invoices are entered into the ERP by hand.
automation.problem2Text: Order confirmations, follow-ups and status queries live in email — and get stuck there.
automation.problem3Text: Quality documents, inspection reports and minutes are produced and filed by hand.
automation.problemFooter: These are not IT problems. These are process problems, solvable with the right tools.
automation.step1Desc: Half a day on site. I look at where the manual bridges are and identify the process with the biggest lever.
automation.step2Desc: In 1–2 weeks I build a working prototype. You test it with real data and give feedback.
automation.step3Desc: After tweaks the automation goes to production. Includes 30 days of support.
automation.aboutMiniText: Computer-science degree at UZH, professional experience at UBS and KPMG, club president for 200 members. I understand both the technology and the everyday life of organisations.
automation.bottomCtaHeading: Let's spend 20 minutes finding out which process gives you the most leverage.

scan.bannerBody: This scanner is a visual preview. No data is sent or stored.
scan.step1HeaderTitle: Which processes have the biggest automation potential?
scan.step1HeaderSub: Click through a few targeted questions in 5 minutes — at the end you'll see a sample report that illustrates the real analysis.
scan.step1Trust1: This preview runs entirely in your browser — no data is sent.
scan.step3HeaderSub: In the real version this is where your contact details go. In this preview everything stays in your browser — nothing is sent or stored. The sample report follows on the next step.
scan.step3Consent: I understand this is a demo and that no data is processed or stored.
scan.step4DeepDesc: Personally reviewed, with industry research and a concrete action plan. Turnaround 1–2 working days, free.
